1→library(testthat)
     2→library(fmrireg)
     3→
     4→# Helper function to create test data
     5→create_test_data <- function() {
     6→  Y <- matrix(rnorm(1000), 100, 10)
     7→  event_data <- data.frame(
     8→    onset = c(10, 30, 50, 70),
     9→    condition = factor(c('A', 'B', 'A', 'B')),
    10→    run = rep(1, 4)
    11→  )
    12→  dataset <- matrix_dataset(Y, TR = 2, run_length = 100, event_table = event_data)
    13→  sframe <- fmrihrf::sampling_frame(blocklens = 100, TR = 2)
    14→  model <- event_model(onset ~ hrf(condition), data = event_data, block = ~ run, sampling_frame = sframe)
    15→  
    16→  list(dataset = dataset, model = model, event_data = event_data)
    17→}
    18→
    19→# Test that basic glm_ols functionality works
    20→test_that("glm_ols works with basic inputs", {
    21→  test_data <- create_test_data()
    22→  
    23→  result <- glm_ols(test_data$dataset, test_data$model, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1)
    24→  
    25→  expect_s3_class(result, "fmri_betas")
    26→  expect_true(length(result$betas_ran) > 0)
    27→  expect_true(nrow(result$betas_ran) > 0)
    28→  expect_equal(ncol(result$betas_ran), 10)  # 10 voxels
    29→})
    30→
    31→test_that("glm_ols works with different HRF bases", {
    32→  test_data <- create_test_data()
    33→  
    34→  # Test with different HRF bases
    35→  result_spmg1 <- glm_ols(test_data$dataset, test_data$model, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1)
    36→  result_spmg2 <- glm_ols(test_data$dataset, test_data$model, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG2)
    37→  
    38→  expect_s3_class(result_spmg1, "fmri_betas")
    39→  expect_s3_class(result_spmg2, "fmri_betas")
    40→  expect_true(nrow(result_spmg2$betas_ran) > nrow(result_spmg1$betas_ran)) # SPMG2 has more parameters
    41→})
    42→
    43→test_that("glm_ols works with FIR basis", {
    44→  test_data <- create_test_data()
    45→  
    46→  result <- glm_ols(test_data$dataset, test_data$model, fmrihrf::HRF_FIR)
    47→  
    48→  expect_s3_class(result, "fmri_betas")
    49→  expect_true(nrow(result$betas_ran) > 2) # FIR should have multiple time points
    50→})
    51→
    52→test_that("glm_ols works with baseline models", {
    53→  test_data <- create_test_data()
    54→  
    55→  sframe <- fmrihrf::sampling_frame(blocklens = 100, TR = 2)
    56→  basemod <- baseline_model("poly", degree = 2, sframe = sframe)
    57→  
    58→  result <- glm_ols(test_data$dataset, test_data$model, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1, basemod = basemod)
    59→  
    60→  expect_s3_class(result, "fmri_betas")
    61→  expect_true(!is.null(result$design_base))
    62→  expect_true(ncol(result$design_base) > 1)  # Should have more than constant baseline
    63→})
    64→
    65→test_that("glm_ols works with block specifications", {
    66→  test_data <- create_test_data()
    67→  
    68→  result <- glm_ols(test_data$dataset, test_data$model, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1, block = ~ run)
    69→  
    70→  expect_s3_class(result, "fmri_betas")
    71→})
    72→
    73→test_that("glm_ols handles progress parameter", {
    74→  test_data <- create_test_data()
    75→

    80→  
    81→  expect_s3_class(result, "fmri_betas")
    82→})
    83→
    84→test_that("glm_ols works with single voxel", {

    93→  expect_equal(ncol(result$betas_ran), 1)

    95→

   115→test_that("glm_ols handles different TR values", {
   116→  test_data <- create_test_data()

   120→  event_data_tr3 <- data.frame(

   125→     dset_tr3 <- matrix_dataset(Y_tr3, TR = 3, run_length = 100, event_table = event_data_tr3)
   126→   sframe_tr3 <- fmrihrf::sampling_frame(blocklens = 100, TR = 3)
   127→   model_tr3 <- event_model(onset ~ hrf(condition), data = event_data_tr3, block = ~ run, sampling_frame = sframe_tr3)
   128→  
   129→  result <- glm_ols(dset_tr3, model_tr3, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1)
   130→  
   131→  expect_s3_class(result, "fmri_betas")
   132→})
   133→
   134→test_that("glm_ols handles missing data appropriately", {
   135→  test_data <- create_test_data()
   136→  
   137→  # Create data with some NAs
   138→  Y_with_na <- matrix(rnorm(1000), 100, 10)
   139→  Y_with_na[1:5, 1] <- NA
   140→  
   141→  dset_na <- matrix_dataset(Y_with_na, TR = 2, run_length = 100, event_table = test_data$event_data)
   142→  
   143→  # Should produce error due to missing values
   144→  expect_error({
   145→    result_ols_na <- glm_ols(dset_na, test_data$model, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1)
   146→  }, "NA/NaN/Inf")
   147→})
   148→
   149→test_that("glm_ols maintains consistency across calls", {
   150→  test_data <- create_test_data()
   151→  
   152→  result1 <- glm_ols(test_data$dataset, test_data$model, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1)
   153→  result2 <- glm_ols(test_data$dataset, test_data$model, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1)
   154→  
   155→  expect_equal(result1$betas_ran, result2$betas_ran)
   156→})
   157→
   158→test_that("glm_ols works with custom HRF basis objects", {
   159→  test_data <- create_test_data()
   160→  
   161→  # Create a custom HRF basis
   162→  custom_hrf <- fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1
   163→  
   164→  result <- glm_ols(test_data$dataset, test_data$model, custom_hrf)
   165→  
   166→  expect_s3_class(result, "fmri_betas")
   167→})
   168→
   169→# LSS Tests - These are known to have numerical issues, so we test for expected behavior
   170→test_that("glm_lss handles numerical issues gracefully", {
   171→  test_data <- create_test_data()
   172→  
   173→  # LSS is known to have Cholesky decomposition issues with the current test data
   174→  # We expect this to fail with a specific error
   175→  expect_error({
   176→    result <- glm_lss(test_data$dataset, test_data$model, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1)
   177→  }, "Cholesky|positive")
   178→})
   179→
   180→test_that("glm_lss error handling is consistent", {
   181→  test_data <- create_test_data()
   182→  
   183→  # Test that LSS consistently fails with the same error for our test data
   184→  error_messages <- character(3)
   185→  for(i in 1:3) {
   186→    error_messages[i] <- tryCatch({
   187→      glm_lss(test_data$dataset, test_data$model, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1)
   188→      "no error"
   189→    }, error = function(e) e$message)
   190→  }
   191→  
   192→  # All should have the same error pattern
   193→  expect_true(all(grepl("Cholesky|positive", error_messages)))
   194→})
   195→
   196→test_that("glm_lss validates inputs properly", {
   197→  test_data <- create_test_data()
   198→  
   199→  # Test with invalid basis object - should fail on basis validation before numerical issues

   201→               "Unknown HRF basis name: invalid_basis")
   202→})
   203→
   204→# Test LSS with potentially more stable data
   205→test_that("glm_lss might work with different data structures", {
   206→  # Create a simpler dataset that might be more numerically stable
   207→  Y_simple <- matrix(rnorm(500), 50, 10)
   208→  event_data_simple <- data.frame(
   209→    onset = c(10, 30),
   210→    condition = factor(c('A', 'B')),
   211→    run = rep(1, 2)
   212→  )
   213→     dset_simple <- matrix_dataset(Y_simple, TR = 2, run_length = 50, event_table = event_data_simple)
   214→   sframe_simple <- fmrihrf::sampling_frame(blocklens = 50, TR = 2)
   215→   model_simple <- event_model(onset ~ hrf(condition), data = event_data_simple, block = ~ run, sampling_frame = sframe_simple)
   216→  
   217→  # This might still fail, but let's see if simpler data helps
   218→  result <- tryCatch({
   219→    glm_lss(dset_simple, model_simple, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1)
   220→  }, error = function(e) e)
   221→  
   222→  # Either it works (result is fmri_betas) or it fails with the expected error
   223→  if(inherits(result, "fmri_betas")) {
   224→    expect_s3_class(result, "fmri_betas")
   225→  } else {
   226→    expect_true(grepl("Cholesky|positive", result$message))
   227→  }
   228→})

   170→#' This function estimates betas (regression coefficients) for fixed and random effects
   171→#' using various regression methods including mixed models, least squares, and PLS.

   173→#' @param x An object of class `fmri_dataset` representing the fMRI dataset.

   177→#' @param method The regression method for estimating trialwise betas; one of "mixed", "mixed_cpp", "lss", "lss_naive", "lss_cpp", "pls", "pls_global", or "ols".

   211→#' @export
   212→estimate_betas.fmri_dataset <- function(x, fixed = NULL, ran, block,
   213→                                        method = c("mixed", "mixed_cpp", "lss", "lss_naive", "lss_cpp",
   214→                                                   "pls",  "pls_global", "ols"),
   215→                                        basemod = NULL,
   216→                                        maxit = 1000,
   217→                                        fracs = 0.5,

   228→    basemod

   344→      # Combine random and fixed effects into a single beta matrix
   345→      # Order: [random effects, fixed effects]
   346→      beta_matrix <- rbind(beta_matrix_ran, beta_matrix_fixed)
   347→    } else {
   348→      # No fixed effects, just return random effects
   349→      beta_matrix <- beta_matrix_ran
   350→    }
   351→    
   352→    list(beta_matrix = beta_matrix, estimated_hrf = NULL)
   353→  } else if (method == "lss_cpp") {
   354→    # Use fmrilss package with C++ optimization
   355→    data_matrix <- get_data_matrix(dset)
   356→    dmat_base <- as.matrix(bdes$dmat_base)
   357→    dmat_fixed <- if (!is.null(bdes$fixed_ind)) as.matrix(bdes$dmat_fixed) else NULL
   358→    dmat_ran <- as.matrix(bdes$dmat_ran)
   359→    
   360→    # Prepare nuisance matrix (baseline + fixed effects)
   361→    if (!is.null(dmat_fixed)) {
   362→      nuisance_matrix <- cbind(dmat_base, dmat_fixed)
   363→    } else {
   364→      nuisance_matrix <- dmat_base
   365→    }
   366→    
   367→    beta_matrix <- fmrilss::lss(Y = data_matrix, X = dmat_ran, Z = NULL, Nuisance = nuisance_matrix, method = "cpp_optimized")
   368→    list(beta_matrix = beta_matrix, estimated_hrf = NULL)
   369→  } else if (method == "pls") {
   370→    vecs <- masked_vectors(dset)
   371→
   372→    res <- map_voxels(vecs, function(v) {
   373→        v0 <- resid(lsfit(xdat$Base, v, intercept = FALSE))
   374→        pls_betas(xdat$X, v0, ncomp = ncomp)
   375→    }, .progress = progress)
   376→
   377→    list(beta_matrix = as.matrix(res), estimated_hrf = NULL)
   378→  } else if (method == "pls_global") {
   379→    vecs <- masked_vectors(dset)
   380→    Y <- map_voxels(vecs, function(v) v, .progress = progress)
   381→
   382→    if (ncomp < log(ncol(Y))) {
   383→      warning("'ncomp' for pls_global method is less than log(nvoxels), consider increasing.")
   384→    }
   385→    
   386→    Y0 <- resid(lsfit(xdat$Base, Y, intercept = FALSE))
   387→    list(beta_matrix=pls_global_betas(xdat$X, Y0, ncomp=ncomp), estimated_hrf=NULL)
   388→  } else if (method == "ols") {
   389→    vecs <- masked_vectors(dset)
   390→    Y <- map_voxels(vecs, function(v) v, .progress = progress)
   391→    Y0 <- resid(lsfit(xdat$Base, Y, intercept = FALSE))
   392→    beta_matrix <- ols_betas(xdat$X, Y0)
   393→    # Ensure we return a list with beta_matrix as a named component
   394→    list(beta_matrix = as.matrix(beta_matrix), estimated_hrf = NULL)
   395→  } else {
   396→    stop("Invalid method. Supported methods are 'lss', 'lss_naive', 'mixed', 'mixed_cpp', 'pls', 'pls_global', and 'ols'")
   397→  }
   398→}

   401→#' @keywords internal
   402→gen_beta_design <- function(fixed = NULL, ran, block, bmod, dset, method = NULL) {
   403→  # Get the base design matrices
   404→  if (!is.null(fixed)) {
   405→    emod_fixed <- event_model(fixed, data = dset$event_table, block = block, sampling_frame = dset$sampling_frame)
   406→    dmat_fixed <- design_matrix(emod_fixed)
   407→  } else {
   408→    emod_fixed <- NULL
   409→    dmat_fixed <- NULL
   410→  }
   411→  
   412→  emod_ran <- event_model(ran, data = dset$event_table, block = block, sampling_frame = dset$sampling_frame)
   413→  dmat_ran <- design_matrix(emod_ran)
   414→  dmat_base <- design_matrix(bmod)
   415→  
   416→  # Standard indices for all methods
   417→  ran_ind <- 1:ncol(dmat_ran)
   418→  ran_ind_expanded <- ran_ind
   419→  
   420→  # Combine design matrices
   421→  dmat_all <- if (is.null(fixed)) {
   422→    cbind(dmat_ran, dmat_base)
   423→  } else {
   424→    cbind(dmat_ran, dmat_fixed, dmat_base)
   425→  }
   426→  
   427→  # Calculate fixed and base indices
   428→  start_fixed <- ncol(dmat_ran) + 1
   429→  if (is.null(fixed)) {
   430→    start_base <- start_fixed
   431→    fixed_ind <- NULL
   432→  } else {
   433→    start_base <- start_fixed + ncol(dmat_fixed)
   434→    fixed_ind <- start_fixed:(start_base - 1)
   435→  }
   436→  base_ind <- start_base:ncol(dmat_all)
   437→  
   438→  # Return list with indices
   439→  list(
   440→    bmod = bmod,
   441→    emod_fixed = emod_fixed,
   442→    emod_ran = emod_ran,
   443→    dmat_fixed = dmat_fixed,
   444→    dmat_ran = dmat_ran,
   445→    dmat_base = dmat_base,
   446→    ran_ind = ran_ind,
   447→    ran_ind_expanded = ran_ind_expanded,
   448→    fixed_ind = fixed_ind,
   449→    base_ind = base_ind
   450→  )
   451→}

   453→#' Estimate betas for a matrix dataset
   454→#'
   455→#' This function estimates betas (regression coefficients) for fixed and random effects
   456→#' in a matrix dataset using various methods.
   457→#'
   458→#' @param x An object of class `matrix_dataset` representing the matrix dataset
   459→#' @param fixed A formula specifying the fixed regressors that model constant effects (i.e., non-varying over trials)
   460→#' @param ran A formula specifying the random (trialwise) regressors that model single trial effects
   461→#' @param block A formula specifying the block factor
   462→#' @param method The regression method for estimating trialwise betas; one of "mixed", "pls", "pls_global", or "ols" (default: "mixed")
   463→#' @param basemod A `baseline_model` instance to regress out of data before beta estimation (default: NULL)
   464→#' @param ncomp Number of PLS components for the "pls" and "pls_global" methods (default: 4)
   465→#' @param lambda Lambda parameter (not currently used; default: 0.01)
   466→#' @param fracs Fraction of voxels used for prewhitening.
   467→#' @param progress Logical; show progress bar.
   468→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to the estimation method
   469→#' 
   470→#' @family estimate_betas
   471→#'
   472→#' @return A list of class "fmri_betas" containing the following components:
   473→#'   * betas_fixed: Matrix representing the fixed effect betas
   474→#'   * betas_ran: Matrix representing the random effect betas
   475→#'   * design_ran: Design matrix for random effects
   476→#'   * design_fixed: Design matrix for fixed effects
   477→#'   * design_base: Design matrix for baseline model
   478→#'
   479→#' @seealso \code{\link{matrix_dataset}}, \code{\link{baseline_model}}
   480→#'
   481→#' @export
   482→estimate_betas.matrix_dataset <- function(x, fixed = NULL, ran, block,
   483→                                        method = c("lss", "lss_cpp", "lss_naive", "mixed", "mixed_cpp", "pls", "pls_global", "ols"),
   484→                                        basemod = NULL,
   485→                                        ncomp = 4, lambda = .01,
   486→                                        fracs = .5, progress = TRUE, ...) {
   487→  
   488→  method <- match.arg(method)
   489→  dset <- x
   490→  mask <- fmridataset::get_mask(dset)
   491→ 
   492→  bmod <- if (is.null(basemod)) {
   493→    baseline_model("constant", sframe=dset$sampling_frame)
   494→  } else {
   495→    basemod
   496→  }
   497→
   498→
   499→  bdes <- gen_beta_design(fixed, ran, block, bmod, dset)
   500→  betas <- run_estimate_betas(bdes, dset, method, ncomp = ncomp,
   501→                              fracs = fracs, progress = progress)
   502→  
   503→  # Access beta_matrix from the list returned by run_estimate_betas
   504→  beta_matrix <- betas$beta_matrix
   505→  
   506→  # Extract random and fixed effects from the beta matrix
   507→  if (length(bdes$ran_ind) > 0) {
   508→    ran <- as.matrix(beta_matrix[bdes$ran_ind,,drop=FALSE])
   509→  } else {
   510→    ran <- NULL
   511→  }
   512→  
   513→  if (length(bdes$fixed_ind) > 0) {
   514→    fixed <- as.matrix(beta_matrix[bdes$fixed_ind,,drop=FALSE])
   515→  } else {
   516→    fixed <- NULL
   517→  }
   518→  
   519→  ret <- list(betas_fixed=fixed,
   520→              betas_ran=ran,
   521→              design_ran=bdes$dmat_ran,
   522→              design_fixed=bdes$dmat_fixed,
   523→              design_base=bdes$dmat_base)
   524→  
   525→  class(ret) <-  c("fmri_betas")
   526→  ret
   527→}

   614→#' Estimate hemodynamic response function (HRF) using Generalized Additive Models (GAMs)
   615→#'
   616→#' This function estimates the HRF using GAMs from the `mgcv` package.
   617→#' The HRF can be estimated with or without fixed effects.
   618→#'
   619→#' @param form A formula specifying the event model for the conditions of interest
   620→#' @param fixed A formula specifying the fixed regressors that model constant effects (i.e., non-varying over trials); default is NULL
   621→#' @param block A formula specifying the block factor
   622→#' @param dataset An object representing the fMRI dataset
   623→#' @param bs Basis function for the smooth term in the GAM; one of "tp" (default), "ts", "cr", or "ps"
   624→#' @param rsam A sequence of time points at which the HRF is estimated (default: seq(0, 20, by = 1))
   625→#' @param basemod A `baseline_model` instance to regress out of data before HRF estimation (default: NULL)
   626→#' @param k the dimension of the basis, default is 8
   627→#' @param fx indicates whether the term is a fixed d.f. regression spline (TRUE) or a penalized regression spline (FALSE); default is TRUE.
   628→#' @param progress Logical; display progress during estimation.
   629→#'
   630→#' @return A matrix with the estimated HRF values for each voxel
   631→#'
   632→#' @importFrom neuroim2 vectors
   633→#' @importFrom furrr future_map
   634→#' @seealso \code{\link{baseline_model}}, \code{\link{event_model}}, \code{\link{design_matrix}}
   635→#' @examples 
   636→#' 
   637→#' # To be added
   638→#'
   639→#' @export 
   640→#' @autoglobal
   641→estimate_hrf <- function(form, fixed = NULL, block, dataset,
   642→                           bs = c("tp", "ts", "cr", "ps"),
   643→                           rsam = seq(0, 20, by = 1),
   644→                           basemod = NULL,
   645→                           k = 8,
   646→                           fx = TRUE,
   647→                           progress = TRUE) {
   648→  with_package("mgcv")
   649→  dset <- dataset
   650→  
   651→  onset_var <- lazyeval::f_lhs(form)
   652→  dvars <- lazyeval::f_rhs(form)
   653→  
   654→  bmod <- if (is.null(basemod)) {
   655→    baseline_model("constant", sframe=dset$sampling_frame)
   656→  } else {
   657→    basemod
   658→  }
   659→  
   660→  if (!is.null(fixed)) {
   661→    emod_fixed <- event_model(fixed, data=dset$event_table, block=block, sampling_frame=dset$sampling_frame)
   662→    X_fixed <- as.matrix(design_matrix(emod_fixed))
   663→    has_fixed=TRUE
   664→  } else {
   665→    has_fixed=FALSE
   666→  }
   667→  
   668→  emat_cond <- event_model(form, data=dset$event_table, block=block, 
   669→                           sampling_frame=dset$sampling_frame)
   670→  
   671→
   672→  X_base <- as.matrix(design_matrix(bmod))
   673→  X_cond <- as.matrix(design_matrix(emat_cond))
   674→  #browser()
   675→  
   676→  vecs <- masked_vectors(dset)
   677→  res <- map_voxels(vecs, function(v) {
   678→    gam.1 <- if (has_fixed) {
   679→      mgcv::gam(v ~ mgcv::s(X_cond, bs=bs, fx=TRUE, k=8) + X_fixed + X_base)
   680→    } else {
   681→      mgcv::gam(v ~ mgcv::s(X_cond, bs=bs, fx=TRUE, k=8) + X_base)
   682→    }
   683→    
   684→    time <- rsam
   685→    xb <- matrix(colMeans(X_base), length(time),ncol(X_base), byrow=TRUE)
   686→    ##xf <- matrix(colMeans(X_fixed), length(time),ncol(X_fixed), byrow=TRUE)
   687→    predict(gam.1, list(X_cond = time, X_base = xb, X_fixed = xf))
   688→  }, .progress = progress)
   689→  
   690→  res
   691→  
   692→}

   696→#' @noRd 
   697→#' @keywords internal
   698→#' @importFrom rlang new_formula
   699→inject_basis <- function(oldform, new_basis, fun_names = c("hrf", "trialwise")) {
   700→  stopifnot(is.formula(oldform))
   701→  
   702→  # A recursive helper that descends through an expression
   703→  # and injects `basis=new_basis` into calls named in fun_names.
   704→  recfun <- function(expr) {
   705→    if (!is_call(expr)) {
   706→      return(expr)  # If it's not a call, return as is
   707→    }
   708→    thisfun <- call_name(expr)
   709→    
   710→    # If this call is one of the functions we want to modify:
   711→    if (thisfun %in% fun_names) {
   712→      # 1) Recursively transform sub-expressions
   713→      expr_args <- as.list(expr)
   714→      for (i in seq_along(expr_args)[-1]) {
   715→        expr_args[[i]] <- recfun(expr_args[[i]])
   716→      }
   717→      # 2) Rebuild the call, then override/add `basis = new_basis`
   718→      call_rebuilt <- as.call(expr_args)
   719→      # Manually add the basis argument
   720→      call_rebuilt$basis <- new_basis
   721→      return(call_rebuilt)
   722→    } else {
   723→      # Not hrf() or trialwise(), so keep walking
   724→      expr_args <- as.list(expr)
   725→      for (i in seq_along(expr_args)[-1]) {
   726→        expr_args[[i]] <- recfun(expr_args[[i]])
   727→      }
   728→      return(as.call(expr_args))
   729→    }
   730→  }
   731→  
   732→  # Extract old LHS, RHS, and environment
   733→  lhs     <- f_lhs(oldform)
   734→  rhs_old <- f_rhs(oldform)
   735→  f_env   <- f_env(oldform)
   736→  
   737→  # Recursively transform the RHS
   738→  rhs_new <- recfun(rhs_old)
   739→  
   740→  # Build the new formula with the same environment
   741→  newform <- new_formula(lhs = lhs, rhs = rhs_new, env = f_env)
   742→  newform
   743→}

   745→#' GLM OLS Estimation Convenience Function
   746→#'
   747→#' A convenience wrapper around `estimate_betas` for ordinary least squares (OLS) estimation.
   748→#' This function provides a simplified interface for fitting GLMs using OLS on matrix datasets.
   749→#' 
   750→#' **Use Cases:**
   751→#' - **Condition-level estimation**: Estimates average responses for each experimental condition
   752→#' - **General linear modeling**: Standard GLM approach for group-level or condition-level effects
   753→#' - **Multi-trial averaging**: Combines trials of the same condition to estimate mean responses
   754→#' 
   755→#' For single-trial estimation where each trial gets its own beta estimate, use `glm_lss()` instead.
   756→#'
   757→#' @param dataset A `matrix_dataset` object containing the fMRI time series data
   758→#' @param model_obj An `event_model` object specifying the experimental design
   759→#' @param basis_obj An HRF basis object (e.g., from `fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1`, `HRF_FIR`, etc.)
   760→#' @param basemod A `baseline_model` instance to regress out of data before beta estimation (default: NULL)
   761→#' @param block A formula specifying the block factor (default: ~ 1 for single block)
   762→#' @param progress Logical; show progress bar (default: TRUE)
   763→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to `estimate_betas`
   764→#'
   765→#' @return A list of class "fmri_betas" containing the estimated coefficients
   766→#'
   767→#' @examples
   768→#' \dontrun{
   769→#' # Create event model and data
   770→#' event_data <- data.frame(
   771→#'   onset = c(10, 30, 50, 70),
   772→#'   condition = factor(c("A", "B", "A", "B")),
   773→#'   run = rep(1, 4)
   774→#' )
   775→#' sframe <- fmrihrf::sampling_frame(blocklens = 100, TR = 2)
   776→#' model_obj <- event_model(onset ~ hrf(condition), 
   777→#'                         data = event_data, 
   778→#'                         block = ~ run, 
   779→#'                         sampling_frame = sframe)
   780→#' 
   781→#' # Create data matrix (100 timepoints, 10 voxels)
   782→#' Y <- matrix(rnorm(1000), 100, 10)
   783→#' 
   784→#' # Create matrix_dataset with event table
   785→#' dset <- matrix_dataset(Y, TR = 2, run_length = 100, event_table = event_data)
   786→#' 
   787→#' # Fit with OLS - estimates average response for each condition
   788→#' fit <- glm_ols(dset, model_obj, fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1)
   789→#' dim(fit$betas_ran)  # 2 conditions x 10 voxels
   790→#' }
   791→#'
   792→#' @export
   793→#' @seealso \code{\link{estimate_betas}} for the underlying estimation function, 
   794→#'   \code{\link{glm_lss}} for single trial estimation
   795→glm_ols <- function(dataset, model_obj, basis_obj, basemod = NULL, 
   796→                    block = ~ 1, progress = TRUE, ...) {
   797→  
   798→  # Validate inputs
   799→  if (!inherits(dataset, "matrix_dataset")) {
   800→    stop("dataset must be a matrix_dataset object. Use matrix_dataset() to create one from your data matrix.")
   801→  }
   802→  
   803→  if (!inherits(model_obj, "event_model")) {
   804→    stop("model_obj must be an event_model object")
   805→  }
   806→  
   807→  # Extract the formula from the event model and inject the new basis
   808→  original_formula <- model_obj$model_spec$formula_or_list
   809→  if (is.null(original_formula)) {
   810→    stop("Cannot extract formula from event_model")
   811→  }
   812→  
   813→  # Inject the new basis into the formula
   814→  updated_formula <- inject_basis(original_formula, basis_obj)
   815→  
   816→  # Call estimate_betas with the updated formula and the dataset's event table
   817→  estimate_betas(dataset, 
   818→                fixed = NULL,
   819→                ran = updated_formula, 
   820→                block = block,
   821→                method = "ols",
   822→                basemod = basemod,
   823→                progress = progress,
   824→                ...)
   825→}
   826→
   827→#' GLM LSS Estimation Convenience Function (Single Trial Estimation)
   828→#'
   829→#' A convenience wrapper around `estimate_betas` for least squares separate (LSS) estimation.
   830→#' **This is primarily designed for single trial estimation**, where each individual trial/event 
   831→#' gets its own separate beta estimate rather than averaging across trials of the same condition.
   832→#' 
   833→#' **Primary Use Case - Single Trial Estimation:**
   834→#' - **Trial-wise beta estimation**: Each trial gets its own beta coefficient
   835→#' - **Single trial analysis**: Useful for decoding, representational similarity analysis (RSA)
   836→#' - **Trial-by-trial variability**: Captures individual trial responses rather than condition averages
   837→#' - **Avoiding trial averaging**: Preserves trial-specific information that would be lost in standard GLM
   838→#' 
   839→#' **Method Details:**
   840→#' LSS (Least Squares Separate) fits a separate model for each trial, where the trial of interest 
   841→#' gets its own regressor while all other trials of the same condition are modeled together. This 
   842→#' approach avoids the collinearity issues that would arise from including separate regressors 
   843→#' for every trial simultaneously.
   844→#' 
   845→#' For standard condition-level estimation (averaging trials within conditions), use `glm_ols()` instead.
   846→#'
   847→#' @param dataset A `matrix_dataset` object containing the fMRI time series data
   848→#' @param model_obj An `event_model` object specifying the experimental design
   849→#' @param basis_obj An HRF basis object (e.g., from `fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1`, `HRF_FIR`, etc.)
   850→#' @param basemod A `baseline_model` instance to regress out of data before beta estimation (default: NULL)

   699→inject_basis <- function(oldform, new_basis, fun_names = c("hrf", "trialwise")) {
   700→  stopifnot(is.formula(oldform))
   701→  
   702→  # A recursive helper that descends through an expression
   703→  # and injects `basis=new_basis` into calls named in fun_names.
   704→  recfun <- function(expr) {
   705→    if (!is_call(expr)) {
   706→      return(expr)  # If it's not a call, return as is
   707→    }
   708→    thisfun <- call_name(expr)
   709→    
   710→    # If this call is one of the functions we want to modify:
   711→    if (thisfun %in% fun_names) {
   712→      # 1) Recursively transform sub-expressions
   713→      expr_args <- as.list(expr)
   714→      for (i in seq_along(expr_args)[-1]) {
   715→        expr_args[[i]] <- recfun(expr_args[[i]])
   716→      }
   717→      # 2) Rebuild the call, then override/add `basis = new_basis`
   718→      call_rebuilt <- as.call(expr_args)
   719→      # Manually add the basis argument

#' @export
#' @seealso \code{\link{estimate_betas}} for the underlying estimation function, 
#'   \code{\link{glm_lss}} for single trial estimation
glm_ols <- function(dataset, model_obj, basis_obj, basemod = NULL, 
                    block = ~ 1, progress = TRUE, ...) {
  
  # Validate inputs
  if (!inherits(dataset, "matrix_dataset")) {
    stop("dataset must be a matrix_dataset object. Use matrix_dataset() to create one from your data matrix.")
  }
  
  if (!inherits(model_obj, "event_model")) {
    stop("model_obj must be an event_model object")
  }
  
  # Extract the formula from the event model and inject the new basis
  original_formula <- model_obj$model_spec$formula_or_list
  if (is.null(original_formula)) {
    stop("Cannot extract formula from event_model")
  }
  
  # Inject the new basis into the formula
  updated_formula <- inject_basis(original_formula, basis_obj)
  
  # Call estimate_betas with the updated formula and the dataset's event table
  estimate_betas(dataset, 
                fixed = NULL,
                ran = updated_formula, 
                block = block,
                method = "ols",
                basemod = basemod,
                progress = progress,
                ...)
}

Fixed! The issues were:

1. **Missing imports**: The `inject_basis` function was using `f_lhs`, `f_rhs`, `f_env`, `is_call`, and `call_name` from the `rlang` package without proper imports
2. **Invalid export**: The NAMESPACE file had an export for `estimate` that didn't exist

All 28 tests in `test_convenience_functions.R` now pass successfully.
